rules.name: Standalone components only
rules.check: Scan all @Component decorators. Flag any without standalone: true or declared in NgModule.
rules.fix: Add standalone: true.

rules.name: Separate files for feature/page components
rules.check: In features/ or pages/, verify templateUrl and styleUrl (not inline). In ui/, inline is acceptable.
rules.why: Complex components with inline templates become unreadable.

rules.name: Concise folder naming
rules.check: Flag folders whose name starts with parent folder name (challenges/challenges-list/ → challenges/list/).

rules.name: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush always
rules.check: Flag any @Component without changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush.
rules.fix: Add changeDetection: ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush.

rules.name: File and class naming conventions
rules.check: Files: kebab-case with type suffix (.component.ts, .store.ts, etc). Classes: PascalCase matching file. Signal vars: no $ suffix. Observable vars: must have $ suffix.

rules.name: Selector prefix handled by ESLint
rules.check: Skip — ESLint handles via @angular-eslint/component-selector.

rules.name: One concern per folder
rules.check: Each distinct concern (component, dialog, service group, interceptor group, guard group) must have its own subfolder. Flag: (1) Two or more components in the same folder — each component gets its own folder with its .ts/.html/.scss/.spec.ts files. (2) Different concern types mixed flat — e.g., interceptors, guards, and services all loose in the same folder instead of grouped into interceptors/, guards/ subfolders. (3) A dialog/modal component living inside a form or page component's folder instead of its own subfolder. Acceptable: a component's .store.ts, .spec.ts, and template/style files co-located in the same folder as the component. Acceptable: a single shared utility file (validators, utils) at the lib root if used by multiple siblings. Acceptable: routes.ts at the lib root.
rules.fix: Move each component into its own named subfolder. Group related non-component files (interceptors, guards) into a named subfolder.
rules.why: Flat folders with mixed concerns make it impossible to find things at a glance. One folder = one responsibility = one mental unit.

rules.name: Signal-based component API only
rules.check: Flag @Input(, @Output(, @ViewChild(, @ViewChildren(, @ContentChild(, @ContentChildren( decorators.
rules.fix: Use input(), output(), viewChild(), viewChildren(), contentChild(), contentChildren(), model().
rules.why: Angular 21 signal API is stable. No exceptions.

rules.name: Signals over RxJS in components
rules.check: In components (not stores/services), flag BehaviorSubject, Subject, combineLatest, merge when signal/computed would suffice.
rules.why: Signals are simpler for non-stream data.

rules.name: No async pipe in templates
rules.check: Flag every | async in .html templates.
rules.fix: Use toSignal() in component, signal in template.

rules.name: Subscription cleanup with takeUntilDestroyed
rules.check: In components, every .subscribe() must have takeUntilDestroyed() before it.
rules.exceptions: rxMethod in stores managed by store lifecycle.

rules.name: No complex logic in templates
rules.check: Flag method calls in interpolations ({{ fn() }}), ternaries, chained access beyond 2 levels.
rules.fix: Extract to computed() signal.
rules.why: Method calls run every change detection cycle.

rules.name: signalStore for shared state and API calls
rules.check: Flag direct API service injection in components. Flag @Input/@Output chains > 2 levels between siblings.
rules.why: Stores centralize state and data flow.

rules.name: withPaginatedList for paginated APIs
rules.check: Stores fetching paginated endpoints must use withPaginatedList. Flag manual pagination state.

rules.name: Store composition order
rules.check: Verify: withState → withComputed → withMethods → custom features.

rules.name: rxMethod pattern: tap(loading) → switchMap → tap(success) → catchError → finalize
rules.check: Every rxMethod with a loading flag must follow this pipe order: (1) tap(() => patchState(store, { saving: true })) BEFORE switchMap to set loading, (2) switchMap expression-only with inner pipe containing tap (success), catchError(() => EMPTY), finalize (cleanup loading). Flag: patchState for loading inside switchMap callback, missing catchError, missing finalize.
rules.fix: Move patchState({ saving: true }) to a tap() operator BEFORE switchMap. catchError must return EMPTY. finalize resets loading.
rules.why: Loading state set inside switchMap couples concerns. tap before switchMap is scannable. Without catchError stream dies. Without finalize spinners stuck.

rules.name: No direct HttpClient injection
rules.check: Flag inject(HttpClient) or HttpClient import anywhere.
rules.fix: Use generated services from @libs/core/api.

rules.name: No .subscribe() in components
rules.check: Flag .subscribe() in components. API/store calls → store rxMethod. UI-only → needs takeUntilDestroyed().

rules.name: No unsafe type casts on API calls
rules.check: Flag 'as any', 'as Observable<any>', 'as unknown' on generated API service calls.
rules.fix: Fix OpenAPI spec and regenerate.

rules.name: No async/await in signalStore methods
rules.check: Inside withMethods of signalStore, flag any async function, firstValueFrom(), lastValueFrom(), or Promise-based API calls. All API calls in stores must use rxMethod with a pipe-based reactive stream.
rules.fix: Replace async method with rxMethod using pipe(switchMap(() => service.call()), tap(result => patchState(...)), catchError(() => EMPTY), finalize(() => patchState(...))).
rules.why: async/await breaks the reactive stream — errors kill the method permanently, no retry, no cancellation, no backpressure. rxMethod keeps the stream alive.

donts.label: Simple API call with firstValueFrom
API call with try/catch state management
Sequential API calls with await chaining

dos.label: Simple API call → rxMethod storing result in state
API call needing store state → map + filter before switchMap
Sequential API calls → switchMap chain with forkJoin for parallel loads

rules.name: Concise catchError syntax
rules.check: Flag catchError with block body that only returns EMPTY: catchError(() => { return EMPTY; }). Must be expression form.
rules.fix: Replace with catchError(() => EMPTY).

rules.name: rxMethod pipe composition: no switchMap block bodies, loading state always in tap before switchMap
rules.check: In rxMethod pipes, flag: (1) switchMap/exhaustMap with block body (=> { ... return ...; }), (2) patchState({ saving/loading: true }) inside switchMap callback instead of in a tap() before switchMap. switchMap must be expression-only (=> service.call().pipe(...)). Side effects (patchState for loading), guards (filter), and store reads (map(() => store.x())) go in separate pipe operators BEFORE switchMap.
rules.fix: Move patchState({ saving: true }) to a tap() step BEFORE switchMap. Move store reads to map() + filter(Boolean) steps before switchMap. switchMap receives the prepared value and returns the API observable directly (expression-only, no block body).
rules.why: Block bodies in switchMap hide side effects and mix concerns. patchState inside switchMap couples loading state management with the API call. Flat pipe steps are scannable — each operator does one thing: tap sets loading, switchMap calls the API, inner tap handles success, catchError handles failure, finalize resets loading.

donts.label: switchMap with block body mixing state, guards, and API call
patchState for loading INSIDE switchMap callback (even without block body)

dos.label: Flat pipe: map → filter → tap → switchMap (expression only)
dos.code:
saveItem: rxMethod<void>(
  pipe(
    map(() => store.item()),
    filter(Boolean),
    tap(() => patchState(store, { saving: true })),
    switchMap((item) =>
      service.update({ id: item.id, body: item }).pipe(
        tap((res) => patchState(store, { item: res })),
        catchError(() => EMPTY),
        finalize(() => patchState(store, { saving: false })),
      ),
    ),
  ),
)

dos.label: Loading state in tap BEFORE switchMap, switchMap is expression-only

rules.name: rxMethod calls require injection context
rules.check: Flag any rxMethod call with a signal or observable argument that happens outside an injection context (constructor, field initializer, or explicit injector). Common violation: calling store.loadSomething(signal) inside ngOnInit or afterNextRender without passing an injector.
rules.fix: Either move the call to the constructor/field initializer, or pass { injector: this.injector } as the second argument to the rxMethod call.
rules.why: ngrx/signals requires injection context for reactive method subscriptions. Calling outside one is deprecated and will throw in future versions.

rules.name: Never navigate before async store action completes
rules.check: Flag any component method that calls a store action (rxMethod) and then calls router.navigate() synchronously afterward. The navigation destroys the component, unsubscribes the observable, and cancels the in-flight HTTP request.
rules.fix: Store method must accept an onSuccess callback. Call router.navigate() inside the callback, which the store invokes inside tap() after the HTTP response succeeds. Pattern: store.delete({ id, onSuccess: () => this.router.navigate([...]) }).
rules.why: Navigating before the HTTP response arrives cancels the request. Deletes, creates, and updates silently fail while the UI navigates away as if they succeeded.

rules.name: All forms extend BaseFormComponent<T>
rules.check: Components with form()/FormField must extend BaseFormComponent<T>.

rules.name: Forms emit save, never handle submission
rules.check: Form components must have output() named save. Flag store/API injection in form components.
rules.why: Forms collect data, pages decide what to do.

rules.name: Validators as factory functions
rules.check: Validators must be function references or factory calls, not inline arrows.

rules.name: Validation: blur, i18n, error below field
rules.check: Every FormField with validators needs error display in template using | translate and ngx-error.

rules.name: Zero hardcoded user-visible text
rules.check: Flag text content in templates, string args to MessageService/ConfirmationService/DynamicDialogConfig not using translate.

rules.name: Translation keys scoped to feature
rules.check: Key prefix must match library (candidates.* in candidates/, common.* anywhere).

rules.name: Every key in both IT and EN JSON
rules.check: Collect keys from code, verify both public/i18n/it.json and en.json have them.
rules.why: Missing key shows raw key string.

rules.name: No orphaned translation keys
rules.check: Keys in JSON not referenced in any template or TypeScript.

rules.name: TranslateModule in every component using translate pipe
rules.check: Template with | translate → @Component.imports must include TranslateModule.

rules.name: Generated API services only
rules.check: API imports must come from @libs/core/api, not relative paths. No direct HttpClient.

rules.name: Error interceptor + store-level handling
rules.check: Every rxMethod has catchError + finalize. Critical flows (invite, eval) have custom error messages.
rules.why: Global interceptor + store-level defense in depth.

rules.name: OpenAPI drift detection (sub-agent)
rules.check: When generated client or API usage in diff → spawn openapi-drift sub-agent.

rules.name: No barrel import bloat
rules.check: Flag imports from library root when deep import available (primeng → primeng/button).

rules.name: No business/UI logic in libs/core/api
rules.check: Flag any hand-written file in libs/core/api/src/lib/ that is NOT inside the generated/ directory. The @libs/core/api library must contain ONLY the generated/ directory (services, models, api-configuration) and the barrel index.ts. Pipes, utility functions, enum configs, severity mappers, and any other business or presentation logic must live in a dedicated lib (e.g., @libs/ui/enum-pipes).
rules.fix: Move the file to the appropriate lib: pipes → @libs/ui/{pipe-name}, domain utils → @libs/core/{domain}. Update all imports.
rules.why: libs/core/api is auto-generated — hand-written code gets overwritten on regeneration and couples presentation logic to the API contract.

donts.label: Severity mapper function in core/api
donts.code:
// libs/core/api/src/lib/status-severity.ts
export function inviteStatusSeverity(status: string): string { ... }

donts.label: Enum config/mapping in core/api
donts.code:
// libs/core/api/src/lib/enums.ts
export const ENUM_CONFIG = { inviteStatus: { names: [...], prefix: 'enums.inviteStatus' } };

dos.label: Centralized enum config with severities
dos.code:
// libs/ui/enum-pipes/src/lib/enum-config.ts
export const ENUM_CONFIG = { inviteStatus: { names: [...], prefix: 'enums.inviteStatus', severities: { Draft: 'secondary', Sent: 'info', ... } } };

dos.label: Generic enumSeverity pipe
core/api barrel exports only generated code

rules.name: Route params via input(), never ActivatedRoute
rules.check: Flag ActivatedRoute import, .snapshot, .paramMap usage.
rules.fix: Use input.required<string>() with withComponentInputBinding().

rules.name: Lazy loading for all page routes
rules.check: Every page route must use loadComponent: () => import(...).

rules.name: Functional guards only
rules.check: Flag class-based CanActivate/CanDeactivate/CanLoad/Resolve.

rules.name: Route path conventions
rules.check: Kebab-case, plural for lists, :id for details.

rules.name: Auth guards on all protected routes
rules.check: Every route except /login must have canActivate with auth guard.

rules.name: Navigation patterns
rules.check: Flag window.location for internal nav. After mutations verify navigation. Verify breadcrumbs.
rules.fix: Use Router.navigate() or routerLink.

rules.name: Navigate to detail after entity creation
rules.check: After a successful create API call (POST that returns an entity Id), the component must navigate to the entity's detail/edit page using router.navigate(['/entity-path', id, 'edit']). Flag create flows that stay on the list page, show only a toast, or close a dialog without navigation. Wizard and standalone create pages must always redirect to the detail page.
rules.why: Users expect to land on the newly created entity to continue editing. Staying on the list forces them to find it manually.

rules.name: Routes via AppRoutes enum only
rules.check: Flag hardcoded route strings in router.navigate(), routerLink, and navItem definitions. All route paths must use AppRoutes enum from @libs/core/routing. For routes with params, use buildRoute(AppRoutes.X, { id }). For simple routes, use AppRoutes.X directly. Flag any string literal that looks like a route path ('/', '/login', '/candidates', etc.) not sourced from the enum.
rules.fix: Import { AppRoutes, buildRoute } from '@libs/core/routing' and replace hardcoded strings.
rules.why: Hardcoded route strings are fragile — renaming a route requires finding every occurrence. The enum provides autocomplete, compile-time safety, and a single source of truth for all route paths.

rules.name: Token access only through auth service
rules.check: Flag localStorage get/set/remove outside auth service. Flag direct Authorization header outside interceptor.

rules.name: No secrets in code
rules.check: Scan for API key formats, JWTs, base64 >40 chars, secret variable names with string literals.

rules.name: Vulnerability scanning
rules.check: Flag [innerHTML], bypassSecurityTrust*, eval(), new Function(), unvalidated URL construction.
rules.why: XSS in admin panel is high-privilege.

rules.name: PrimeNG tree-shake imports
rules.check: Flag primeng root imports. Must use primeng/button, primeng/table, etc.

rules.name: Lazy load heavy PrimeNG components
rules.check: Flag heavy PrimeNG (Dialog, Editor, Calendar) in eagerly loaded components.

rules.name: DynamicDialog for all modals
rules.check: Flag <p-dialog> in templates. Use dialogService.open().

rules.name: Detail page save in page header
rules.check: Save button in <ui-page-header>, not inside form component. Page uses viewChild to call form.onSave().

rules.name: Status badges via enumSeverity pipe
rules.check: Flag inline status displays with hardcoded severity or ternary severity logic. Use p-badge with enumSeverity pipe: [severity]="value | enumSeverity: 'enumType'". Add severities to the enum's ENUM_CONFIG entry in @libs/ui/enum-pipes.
rules.why: Consistent status colors from centralized config.

rules.name: All enum/status values handled in ENUM_CONFIG
rules.check: Compare generated API enum values against ENUM_CONFIG severities mapping. Flag unhandled values. Every value in the enum should have a severity entry.
rules.why: Unhandled status = falls back to default info badge silently.

rules.name: Confirmation for destructive actions
rules.check: Delete/revoke endpoints must show ConfirmationService.confirm() with translated message + entity name.

rules.name: Loading, empty, and error states
rules.check: Every isLoading signal needs template loading indicator. Every list needs empty state. Every fetch needs error state.

rules.name: Toast notifications for mutations
rules.check: After create/update/delete in store tap block, verify MessageService.add() with translate key.

rules.name: Extract non-trivial tab panels into components
rules.check: Each <p-tabpanel> whose content is more than a few simple elements (e.g. contains a form, a table, or business logic) must use a dedicated child component. Only trivially simple tabs (a short paragraph, a single read-only field) may inline their content directly inside <p-tabpanel>. Flag: (1) <p-tabpanel> with >15 lines of template content and no child component. (2) <p-tabpanel> content that injects services, manages signals, or has event handlers. The child component should wrap its content in <ui-tab-content> from @libs/ui/card.
rules.fix: Extract tab content into a standalone component in its own subfolder (e.g. company-info-tab/). The component injects the shared store and handles its own local state. The parent page remains a thin orchestrator: tabs + tab list + child components.
rules.why: Fat tab panels bloat the parent page component, making it hard to read and test. Extracting keeps each tab focused and independently testable.

rules.name: Use full PrimeNG tab structure
rules.check: All tab implementations must use the complete PrimeNG tab structure: <p-tabs> → <p-tablist> → <p-tab> + <p-tabpanels> → <p-tabpanel>. Flag: (1) <p-tabs> without a <p-tabpanels> child — tab content rendered via manual @if blocks outside the <p-tabs> element. (2) <p-tablist> without a sibling <p-tabpanels>. This applies to both local-state tabs (valueChange sets a signal) and routed tabs (valueChange triggers navigation, value comes from route input).
rules.fix: Move tab content inside <p-tabpanels>/<p-tabpanel> within the <p-tabs> element. Set the [value] on each <p-tabpanel> to match its <p-tab>. Remove manual @if (activeTab() === '...') blocks.
rules.why: Manual @if blocks bypass PrimeNG's built-in panel management, break keyboard navigation and ARIA attributes, and create inconsistent tab behavior across the app.

rules.name: Design tokens only
rules.check: Flag hardcoded hex/rgb/named colors (except transparent/inherit/currentColor) and hardcoded font sizes in .scss.
rules.fix: Use PrimeNG tokens, Tailwind theme, or CSS custom properties.

rules.name: No !important without justification
rules.check: Flag !important without comment on same or preceding line.

rules.name: No barrel import bloat
rules.check: Flag library root imports when deep import available.

rules.name: No method calls in templates
rules.check: Flag {{ method() }} and [value]='method()' (event handlers OK).
rules.fix: Use computed() signal.

rules.name: Virtual scroll for large lists
rules.check: Lists >50 items need cdk-virtual-scroll-viewport or PrimeNG virtualScroll.

rules.name: Optimized images
rules.check: Flag without loading='lazy', width, height.

rules.name: Track expression in @for
rules.check: Flag @for without track expression.
rules.why: Without track, Angular recreates all DOM.

rules.name: Libraries must be created via Nx generators
rules.check: Every library under libs/ must be a proper Nx library created with `npx nx g @nx/angular:library`. Flag any library directory under libs/ that is missing a `project.json` file. A manually created directory with just src/index.ts is NOT a valid Nx library.
rules.fix: Run the appropriate Nx generator command for the library type. See the fe-scans/library-structure.md playbook for exact commands per library domain (core, features, pages, ui).
rules.why: Nx generators create project.json, tsconfig files, and register the library in the workspace graph. Without this, the library is invisible to Nx (nx show projects), cannot have its own build/test/lint targets, and breaks dependency graph analysis.

rules.name: Library placement follows domain structure
rules.check: Libraries must be placed under the correct domain: libs/core/ for shared utilities and services, libs/features/ for feature-specific smart components, libs/pages/ for lazy-loaded routed pages, libs/ui/ for presentational UI components. Flag libraries placed at libs/ root or under a wrong domain.
rules.why: Consistent domain grouping makes the codebase navigable and enforces dependency direction (pages → features → core/ui).

rules.name: tsconfig path alias registered
rules.check: Every library must have a corresponding @libs/{domain}/{name} path alias in tsconfig.json pointing to its src/index.ts. Flag libraries without a path alias.
rules.why: Without the path alias, other libraries cannot import from it using the @libs/ prefix.

rules.name: Library must have tsconfig.lib.json and tsconfig.spec.json
rules.check: Every Nx library must have its own tsconfig.lib.json (library compilation config) and tsconfig.spec.json (test compilation config) next to project.json. Flag libraries missing either file.
rules.fix: Run the Nx generator to recreate the library, or manually create the tsconfig files following the pattern from another library.
rules.why: These files define compilation boundaries. Without tsconfig.lib.json, the library cannot be built independently. Without tsconfig.spec.json, tests cannot resolve library-specific paths.

rules.name: Library must have ESLint config
rules.check: Every Nx library must have an eslint.config.mjs (or .eslintrc.json for legacy) file at its root. Flag libraries missing ESLint configuration.
rules.fix: Run the Nx generator which creates the config, or copy from a sibling library and adjust paths.
rules.why: Without per-library ESLint config, `nx lint {lib}` fails and the library is excluded from lint-affected analysis.

rules.name: No deep imports bypassing barrel export
rules.check: Flag any import that reaches into a library's internal path (e.g., `@libs/core/auth/src/lib/auth.service`) instead of using the barrel export (`@libs/core/auth`). Also flag relative imports crossing library boundaries (e.g., `../../core/auth/src/lib/...`).
rules.fix: Import from the barrel: `import { AuthService } from '@libs/core/auth';`. If the symbol is not exported, add it to the library's src/index.ts.
rules.why: Deep imports break encapsulation, bypass the public API, and create fragile coupling to internal file structure. Barrel exports are the library's contract.

rules.name: Library dependency direction enforced
rules.check: Enforce dependency direction: pages -> features -> core, pages -> ui, features -> ui, features -> core. Flag violations: (1) core/ importing from features/ or pages/, (2) ui/ importing from features/ or pages/, (3) features/ importing from pages/, (4) any lib importing from the app shell (frontend/src/).
rules.fix: Move shared code to core/ or ui/. If a feature needs another feature's data, extract the shared part into core/.
rules.why: Backward dependencies create circular coupling, break Nx's affected analysis, and make libraries impossible to use independently.

rules.name: No console/debugger
rules.check: Flag console.log/warn/error/debug/info and debugger.

rules.name: No commented-out code
rules.check: Flag 3+ consecutive commented lines with code patterns.

rules.name: No unused imports/variables/dead code
rules.check: Flag unused imports, variables, private methods, unreachable code.

rules.name: No TODO/FIXME/HACK
rules.check: Flag TODO, FIXME, HACK, XXX, TEMP, WORKAROUND.

rules.name: No any type
rules.check: Flag : any, <any>, as any. Exclude *.spec.ts and generated/.
rules.fix: Use proper types, unknown with guards, or generics.

rules.name: Docs on complex logic only
rules.check: Do NOT flag missing JSDoc. Only flag complex functions without why-comment.

rules.name: Topbar and sidebar always visible
rules.check: Verify shell has topbar+sidebar. Flag display:none/visibility:hidden/@if hiding them. Login exempt.

rules.name: Only content area scrolls
rules.check: Topbar/sidebar fixed or sticky. Main content overflow-y:auto. No body overflow.
rules.why: Fixed shell is admin panel standard.

rules.name: Table headers fixed while scrolling
rules.check: PrimeNG tables: scrollable='true' + scrollHeight. Custom tables: sticky thead.

rules.name: 6-column grid for forms
rules.check: Forms use grid-cols-6. Fields span: numeric=1, medium=2, text=3, long/textarea=6.
rules.why: Divisible by 1,2,3,6. Field size communicates input type.

rules.name: Error display below field
rules.check: Errors positioned after input, red from design tokens, no layout shift.

rules.name: Searchable selects
rules.check: p-select must have [filter]='true' when >10 options.

rules.name: Component reuse before creation
rules.check: Before new UI, search libs/ui/ and PrimeNG for existing match.

rules.name: Font sizes from tokens
rules.check: Flag hardcoded font-size values. Accept var(), Tailwind classes, PrimeNG vars.

rules.name: Colors from tokens
rules.check: Flag hardcoded colors (except transparent/inherit/currentColor).

rules.name: Consistent date format
rules.check: Flag | date:'custom' with hardcoded format. Use shared date pipe.

rules.name: Enums as badges via enumSeverity
rules.check: Flag raw text display of enum values. Use p-badge with enumSeverity pipe: [severity]="value | enumSeverity: 'enumType'". Register severity mappings in ENUM_CONFIG (libs/ui/enum-pipes/src/lib/enum-config.ts). Flag per-enum severity pipes — use the generic EnumSeverityPipe instead.
rules.why: Color coding for scanning. Centralized config prevents per-enum duplication.

rules.name: Spacing from tokens
rules.check: Flag hardcoded px in margin/padding/gap. Use Tailwind or CSS vars.

rules.name: Routed tabs for shareable state
rules.check: Tabs loading different data must use child routes. Flag activeIndex when URL warranted.

rules.name: Table row actions as inline buttons
rules.check: Last column always-visible action buttons. Flag p-menu/dropdown/hover-reveal for row actions.

rules.name: Loading indicators everywhere
rules.check: Every loading signal needs template indicator. Skeletons for content, spinners for blocking ops.

rules.name: Button placement consistency
rules.check: Page actions in ui-page-header right-aligned. Dialog buttons bottom right. Destructive = danger style.

rules.name: Confirm dialogs use correct button labels
rules.check: Delete actions: severity='danger' + acceptLabelKey='common.delete'. Non-destructive confirmations: default severity='primary' + default label 'common.confirm'. Never show 'Delete' for non-delete actions.
rules.why: Button label must match the action to avoid user confusion.

rules.name: Disable controls when action is impossible
rules.check: If the UI can determine upfront that an action will fail (e.g. last owner cannot change role), the control must be [disabled]='true' with a [title] tooltip explaining why. Never let users trigger a known-failing action.
rules.why: Better UX than letting users click and seeing an error.

rules.name: Confirm dialog minimum dimensions
rules.check: Global .p-confirmdialog must have min-width: 420px and min-height: 90px defined in styles.scss.
rules.why: Small dialogs look broken on desktop.

rules.name: PrimeNG as sole UI component library
rules.check: All form inputs, buttons, dialogs, tables, selects, toasts, and badges must use PrimeNG components (pInputText, p-button, p-password, p-select, p-confirmdialog, p-datatable, p-badge, p-toast). Flag custom implementations of components PrimeNG already provides.
rules.why: One library = one look. Mixing libraries creates visual inconsistency.

rules.name: Non-editable fields shown as disabled inputs
rules.check: Entity fields that are not editable (e.g. email on profile/user detail) must be rendered as <input pInputText [disabled]='true'> — never as plain text, spans, or ui-detail-row. Must not be included in backend update commands.
rules.why: Disabled inputs signal 'this field exists but you cannot change it'. Plain text is ambiguous.

rules.name: Tablist pattern for page tabs
rules.check: Page-level tabs must use <p-tabs> with <p-tablist>/<p-tab> + @if blocks for content. Never use <p-tabpanels>/<p-tabpanel>. Tab state via signal or route param.
rules.why: Consistent tab pattern across all pages (invites, challenges, profile).

rules.name: PrimeNG locale synced with ngx-translate
rules.check: PrimeNG translation labels (password strength, etc.) must be synced with ngx-translate via PrimeNG.setTranslation() on language change. Translation keys under 'primeng.*' in i18n files.

rules.name: Global input styles match PrimeNG appearance
rules.check: Global input styles in styles.scss must produce identical look to PrimeNG pInputText. Background: bg-surface-container-lowest. No conflicting borders, focus rings, or placeholder colors between global and PrimeNG styles.
rules.why: Inputs with and without pInputText directive must look the same.

rules.name: PrimeNG component doc registry
rules.check: Before using a PrimeNG component not listed in frontend/PRIMENG.md 'Currently Used Components' table: (1) fetch its docs via WebFetch https://primeng.org/{component}.md, (2) add a row to the table between PRIMENG-TRACKER:START/END markers. Flag new primeng/* imports whose component is missing from PRIMENG.md.
rules.fix: Add the component row to frontend/PRIMENG.md and fetch its docs before using it.
rules.why: The PRIMENG.md registry ensures agents always have doc links for every component in use. Without it, agents guess at API usage and introduce bugs.

rules.name: Images have alt text
rules.check: Flag without alt. Flag generic alt ('image','photo'). Decorative = alt=''.

rules.name: Semantic interactive elements
rules.check: Flag (click) on div/span/p/li/tr. Use <button> or wrap in one.

rules.name: PrimeNG aria labels
rules.check: Icon-only p-button, unlabeled p-inputText/p-select must have ariaLabel.

rules.name: ARCHITECTURE.md for significant libraries
rules.check: Core libraries (libs/core/{lib}), feature libraries (libs/features/{lib}), page libraries (libs/pages/{lib}), and the UI library (libs/ui/) with 3+ source files must have an ARCHITECTURE.md. Flag libraries without one.
rules.why: Embedded architecture docs guide feature developers and code reviewers. Without them, architectural decisions are implicit and easily violated.

rules.name: GUIDELINES.md for significant libraries
rules.check: Same libraries as DOC-001 must have a GUIDELINES.md with coding patterns, conventions, and dos/don'ts specific to that library.
rules.why: Guidelines docs prevent pattern drift and give new contributors concrete examples to follow.

rules.name: ARCHITECTURE.md minimum sections
rules.check: Existing ARCHITECTURE.md files must have at minimum: a heading with module name, a Purpose section, a Components/Structure section, and a Key Decisions section.
rules.why: Skeleton docs with no content are worse than no docs — they create false confidence.

rules.name: GUIDELINES.md minimum sections
rules.check: Existing GUIDELINES.md files must have at minimum: a heading with module name, a Patterns section (what to follow), and a Conventions section (naming, structure).
rules.why: Guidelines must be actionable. A file with only a heading provides no value.
